document.title: Notes Towards a Reducer Core Document
document.purpose: Collect architectural notes, distinctions, questions, terminology, boundaries, candidate outlines, and conceptual discoveries towards a future formal Reducer Core document.
author: Lion Kimbro
Wing-Cat (Chat-GPT)

orientation.summary: This document is not itself the Reducer Core document. It is a staging ground for discoveries, distinctions, architectural principles, unresolved questions, candidate outlines, terminology work, and implementation-oriented thinking related to the Reducer Core architecture.
orientation.goal: Stabilize the conceptual architecture sufficiently that future implementations become more predictable, disciplined, and consciously structured.
orientation.important-note: The purpose is discovery and organization, not premature canonization.

core-discovery.summary: Reducer Core and Blackboard-Judge appear to operate on fundamentally different temporal models.
working-formulation: Blackboard-Judge asks: 'What is happening right now?'
core-discovery.importance: This distinction appears foundational and may become one of the central organizing principles of the eventual architecture.

important-architectural-claim.claim: Reducer events are normalized semantic events, not raw interaction phenomena.

many-inputs-one-semantic-event.interaction: User performs a mouse gesture recognized as quit

important-architectural-claim.important-consequence: Reducer Core becomes independent of the interaction modality that produced the semantic event.

possible-central-principles: Reducer Core consumes semantic events, not raw interaction events.
Not every event deserves semantic historicity.
Reducer state is orientation within reality, not reality itself.
Reducer Core lives in discrete semantic transitions.
Interaction systems recognize meaning; reducers process meaning.
Reducer Core should not become a dumping ground for all state.

semantic-events: What qualifies as a semantic event?
Relationship between semantic events and history.
relationship-to-world-model: Reducer references world state without containing all world data.
relationship-to-blackboard-judge: Why reducers should not directly consume raw interaction streams.

possible-misunderstandings-to-guard-agai: Reducer Core is not merely a place where state changes happen.
Reducer Core is not identical to Redux.
Reducer Core is not the entire application state.
Not every GUI occurrence should become a reducer event.
Reducer Core should not directly process raw pointer movement.
Interaction interpretation should not be collapsed into reducer logic.
Projection should not own semantic truth.
candidate-formulations: Reducer Core is a constitutional engine for semantic transition.
Reducer Core processes normalized semantic declarations.
Reducer Core converts semantic events into lawful session/world transitions.
Reducer Core coordinates workspace orientation within larger reality.
possible-questions: What makes an event semantically meaningful?
Should selection belong to reducer state?
Should reducers emit world mutation commands or world mutation events?
How should reducer history interact with world history?
How much reducer state should be persistent?
What belongs in workspace state versus world state?
Should reducers generate intended world revision IDs?

section-4.title: Reducer State
possible-contents: What should and should not live in reducer state.

merchant-contact-database.importance: Potentially ideal first canonical reducer-core implementation.
reasoning: Good for studying reducer event design.
Good for studying history and persistence.

possible-slogans: Reducer Core lives in semantic transition.
Reducer state is orientation within reality.
Interaction recognizes meaning; reducers process meaning.
Reducer Core consumes semantic declarations, not raw phenomena.
notes: This document should remain flexible and exploratory.
Architectural discoveries should be preserved before cleanup.
Implementation experience should guide later canonization.
